method: Weekly walk-forward, strictly out-of-sample (no fitting). HTF universe = liquid US common $5-75, market cap 300M-20B, vol >=750k, 66 names >=5y history spread across 11 sectors. AMC firing mirrors computeAmcHits + scanOne gates from unified-scanner/engine.ts exactly (scoreAMC>=4 AND green close; fresh<=3 cal days; green>=80; live at as-of price). Outcome: first-touch target(+2.5R)/stop(-1R)/60d-timeout MtM; raw +20/+60d return; excess vs SPY over identical window. De-dupe repeat fires within 10 trading days.

amc.deflatedSharpe: n/a (single pre-specified detector, not best-of-N; per-trade Sharpe ~0.01 is statistically indistinguishable from 0 and the SPY-excess t-stats are strongly NEGATIVE, so no multiple-testing inflation to deflate)
amc.correlationCluster: price-momentum (MACD-hist + EMA-stack + RSI band + VAMI = one momentum vote; redundant with HTF/1Y-return cluster, NOT an independent confirmer)

bottomLine: AMC has NO out-of-sample SPY-relative edge. Across 7,137 setups it underperforms SPY-buy-and-hold at both 20d and 60d horizons (median excess -0.96% / -2.27%; t = -4.8 / -5.2), wins its own 2.5R target only 14% of the time vs the ~28.6% breakeven, and carries a negative clean expectancy (-0.26R). Recommend AMC be DEMOTED from a default green GO in the scanner (same treatment as Rounding Bottom), carrying ~0 weight.
